data.title: Lojas Renner S.A. GAAP EPS of R$0.56
data.description:
• Lojas Renner S.A. press release (LRENY): Q4 GAAP EPS of R$0.56.
  • Retail net revenue increased by 4.3% year-over-year with same store sales growth of 3.3% and a gross margin of 56.5%.
  • Apparel net revenue increased 5.1%, with SSS growth of 4.0%, and apparel gross margin of 57.9% (+0.8 p.p.)
  • Operating expenses increased 2.0%, resulting in a dilution of 0.7 p.p. as a percentage of net revenue from retail operations.
  • Total Adjusted EBITDA reached R$ 1,116 million (+9%), with a margin expansion of 0.9 p.p.
  • Net income totaled a record R$ 553 million (+13%).

data.title: Etsy has the most to gain from OpenAI's Instant Checkout pivot -- BTIG
data.description:
Etsy (ETSY) will likely be the biggest beneficiary of OpenAI’s decision to scale back its Instant Checkout functionality, BTIG’s Marvin Fong says, as the company was the most vulnerable to “agentic disintermediation.” 

This week, OpenAI (OPENAI) said it was moving Instant Checkout out of the ChatGPT app and routing purchases back to the sellers’ app, a development that was immediately celebrated by online retailers, OTAs, and online vehicle marketplaces, and even shoppers who saw little difference between buying directly from the retailer or through Instant Checkout.

“We believe ETSY is the most vulnerable [to Instant Checkout] because it doesn’t touch the product like Amazon or MercadoLibre do, and therefore, can’t offer perks like accelerated delivery or easy returns,” Fong says. With Instant Checkout de-emphasized, “ChatGPT has become much more like any other distribution channel for ETSY, little different from paid search or social.”

“With consumers needing to use the Etsy app, disintermediation risk falls dramatically and loyalty should remain intact,” he adds.

Fong is also bullish on MercadoLibre (MELI) as the OpenAI (OPENAI) news reinforces his confidence in the company’s ability to maintain customer loyalty.

“Even though we viewed the risk of agentic disintermediation for MELI as low due to its fulfillment network and fintech integration, we believe AI has been a significant investor concern over MELI.”

On the other end of the spectrum is RealReal (REAL) given its proprietary supply, which ChatGPT couldn’t bypass.

data.title: Allient signals continued margin expansion and $233M backlog momentum into 2026 supported by data center and defense demand
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Allient Inc. (ALNT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Richard Warzala, Chairman, CEO & President, stated that 2025 closed with "improving momentum across the business" and summarized the quarter as driven by "improving industrial demand, disciplined execution across the organization, and structural margin expansion driven by our Simplify to Accelerate NOW program." He emphasized that the improvement reflected "operating leverage," "improved mix," and sustained cost discipline, with strong trends in industrial and vehicle verticals, and steady medical and aerospace & defense performance. Warzala highlighted that the company delivered "record gross margins for the year," expanded operating income faster than revenue, and generated "record operating cash flow," which contributed to significant net debt reduction. He noted that the company targeted $6 million to $7 million in structural savings for 2025 and made "meaningful progress on that target."
  • James Michaud, Senior VP & CFO, reported, "Fourth quarter revenue increased 17% year-over-year to $143.4 million, including 15% organic growth on a constant currency basis." Michaud detailed that industrial revenue grew 24%, vehicle revenue rose 35%, and medical increased 9%, while aerospace & defense declined 5%. He also stated, "gross margin expanded 90 basis points year-over-year to 32.4%." Michaud added, "operating income increased 76% to $11.4 million or 7.9% of revenue" and "net income for the quarter more than doubled to $6.4 million or $0.38 per diluted share." Adjusted EBITDA reached $19 million or 13.3% of revenue for the quarter, and year-end net debt declined to $139.7 million, reducing the leverage ratio to 1.82x from 3.01x in 2024.

Outlook

  • Warzala noted, "order trends improved" in Q4, with a book-to-bill ratio "slightly above 1x" and backlog ending the year at approximately $233 million, "with the majority expected to convert within 3 to 9 months." He remarked, "as we look into 2026, we believe we are positioned to build on that momentum," while acknowledging that "the macro environment is still uneven across certain end markets" and that policy and tariff considerations remain relevant. Warzala stated, "we continue to align the portfolio around higher-value motion controls and power solutions serving durable secular drivers of electrification, automation, energy efficiency, increased defense spending, and digital infrastructure."

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter revenue was $143.4 million, with 17% year-over-year growth and 15% organic growth. Industrial revenue increased 24%, vehicle revenue increased 35%, and medical rose 9%. Aerospace & defense declined 5%. Gross margin expanded to 32.4%, and operating income grew to $11.4 million. Net income doubled to $6.4 million, adjusted net income reached $9.3 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $19 million. For the full year, net income was $22 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $76.9 million. Cash flow from operations hit $56.7 million, up 35% from the prior year, and inventory turns improved to 3.2x. Net debt declined to $139.7 million, with a leverage ratio of 1.82x.

Q&A

  • Tomohiko Sano, JPMorgan Chase & Co, Research Division: Asked if growth and margin expansion in 2026 would be driven more by cyclical recovery or structural initiatives. Warzala explained, "as we've been repositioning our business...we see some of the long cycle, longer-term drivers. And we mentioned the data center infrastructure. We do see that continuing." He also commented on defense and medical opportunities, emphasizing, "our focus and emphasis on new applications has been in the markets...where the margins are above -- at or above our average."
  • Sano followed up on capital allocation priorities. Warzala replied, "our pipeline of opportunities is quite strong...majority of the investment that we see going forward" will be to support significant current opportunities. He added, "we are paying very close attention...to the pipeline of acquisitions," and the Simplify to Accelerate NOW initiative "will continue."
  • Greg Palm, Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC: Asked about unusual Q4 sequential revenue growth and trends for Q1. Warzala said, "we have a few...pull-ins that we hadn't anticipated" in Q4, particularly in commercial vehicle, and noted, "as we turned the year, we saw that, that was reflected in a little bit lower demand in the first quarter."
  • Palm asked about demand trends and risks from recent events. Warzala responded, "our order inputs seem to be coming in quite well" and discussed defense-related replenishment, stating, "there will need to be an increase in certainly some of the products that we deliver to do some replenishment."
  • Palm inquired about drone market traction. Warzala highlighted, "activity in that market had been primarily in that space, and it accelerated...the pipeline of opportunities go, the prototyping that we're doing, the quoting that we're doing."
  • Palm asked about data center facility expansion and capacity. Warzala replied, "it will be late second quarter, early third quarter when it's fully operational. Timing couldn't have been better...you'll start to see some pretty significant increases in volume in that area."
  • Maxwell Michaelis, Lake Street Capital Markets, LLC: Asked if data center growth would accelerate in 2026. Warzala confirmed, "we do see that continuing...the opportunities are there."
  • Edward Jackson, Northland Capital Markets: Asked about NDAA compliance and supply chain. Warzala answered, "there's always going to be work to be done there...rare earth minerals and materials...are being utilized in some of the higher-performing products here." Michaud added, "the Feds have -- are really investing billions of dollars in a number of companies here in the U.S...it's going to take time for all of the supply chain...to evolve."
  • Jackson asked about the commercial vehicle market. Warzala said, "the onetime effect of the fourth quarter that you could see going forward, I would tell you about $2.5 million," and explained the company's vehicle market mix.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive to slightly positive tone, with several congratulating management and focusing on opportunities in data center, defense, and margin expansion. Questions were probing but constructive, seeking clarity on growth drivers, capital allocation, and supply chain risks.
  • Management’s sentiment was confident and forward-looking in prepared remarks, emphasizing accomplishments and momentum, but became slightly more cautious and detailed in the Q&A, particularly when discussing market volatility, supply chain challenges, and one-time impacts. Warzala stressed ongoing discipline and adaptability: "We will have the discipline to get them done and drive cost out...2026 will not be one that we just sit back and say...let's just take a deep breath...We're going to be aggressively going after some additional opportunities to improve our cost base."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained confident, but with increased emphasis on execution and readiness for uneven markets. Analysts remained supportive but pressed for more granularity on growth sustainability and risk mitigation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to highlight improved backlog and book-to-bill ratio, with management more explicitly addressing the normalization of industrial demand and the anticipated ramp in data center and defense. Strategic focus remained on structural margin gains and diversification, with continued execution of the Simplify to Accelerate NOW program.
  • Analysts’ focus evolved from questions about backlog quality and margin initiatives in Q3 to more detailed inquiries about the sustainability of recent growth, Q1 expectations, and emerging opportunities in defense and data centers.
  • Key metric changes included a sequential revenue increase to $143.4 million, improved gross and operating margins, and further net debt reduction. The tone from management was slightly more tempered, recognizing both the improved financial position and the persistence of market risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited macro uncertainty across end markets, customer capital spending phases, as well as ongoing tariff and policy risks. Supply chain vulnerabilities, particularly regarding rare earth materials and NDAA compliance, were highlighted. Warzala explained ongoing efforts to diversify supply and localize sourcing, while Michaud described active collaboration with suppliers and government. Analysts focused on supply chain compliance, rare earth sourcing, and the sustainability of current demand trends, especially given the one-time nature of some Q4 revenue.

Final Takeaway

Allient’s management closed the year highlighting record financial performance, structural improvements, and a growing backlog that positions the company for continued momentum into 2026. The company emphasized that margin expansion and disciplined cost control are now embedded in its business model, supported by a robust backlog of $233 million and positive order trends. Ongoing initiatives in supply chain localization and facility expansion, particularly for data center and defense markets, are expected to support growth, while management remains vigilant in navigating market volatility and advancing operational efficiencies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: OpenAI rolls out Codex Security agent; cybersecurity stocks show mixed reactions
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) has started rolling out Codex Security, an application security agent designed to help developers identify critical vulnerabilities without being inundated by a barrage of false positives and reports on insignificant bugs.

Unlike when rival Anthropic (ANTHRO) released Claude Code Security last month, prompting an array of cybersecurity stocks to fall, reactions to Codex Security were mixed.

During Friday afternoon trading, CrowdStrike (CRWD) had inched down 0.5%, Cloudflare (NET) had edged up 0.5%, Zscaler (ZS) was relatively static, and Fortinet (FTNT) had declined 1.5%.

Codex Security begins rolling out today in research preview for ChatGPT Enterprise, Business, and Edu subscribers via Codex web. OpenAI is offering it for free over the next month.

"Context is essential when evaluating real security risks, but most AI security tools simply flag low-impact findings and false positives, forcing security teams to spend significant time on triage," OpenAI said. "At the same time, agents are accelerating software development, making security review an increasingly critical bottleneck. Codex Security addresses both challenges. By combining agentic reasoning from our frontier models with automated validation, it delivers high-confidence findings and actionable fixes so teams can focus on the vulnerabilities that matter and ship secure code faster."

The release follows OpenAI's launch of GPT-5.4 Thinking on Thursday, which was designed to help professionals complete tasks such as building spreadsheets, presentations, and documents.

data.title: Microsoft, Google say Anthropic products still available to customers despite Pentagon blacklist
data.description:
Microsoft (MSFT) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) have confirmed that they will keep startup Anthropic’s (ANTHRO) artificial intelligence technology embedded in their products for clients, excluding the U.S. Department of War, CNBC reported. 

Google told the news outlet on Friday, while Microsoft's comments were issued on Thursday.

Earlier on Thursday, the federal agency informed Anthropic that it would label the company a supply-chain risk. Anthropic subsequently said it intends to challenge the move in court.

Last week U.S. President Donald Trump called for federal agencies to drop their use of Anthropic, and Secretary of War Pete Hegseth said Anthropic would keep providing its services to the Pentagon for no more than six months. CNBC has confirmed that Anthropic models played a role in U.S. airstrikes on Iran in recent days.

Microsoft is the first major company to say it will keep working with Anthropic after the Pentagon’s actions. Some defense technology companies have also reportedly told employees to stop using Anthropic’s Claude models and migrate to alternatives.

“Our lawyers have studied the designation and have concluded that Anthropic products, including Claude, can remain available to our customers — other than the Department of War — through platforms such as M365, GitHub, and Microsoft’s AI Foundry and that we can continue to work with Anthropic on non-defense-related projects,” a Microsoft spokesperson told CNBC.

(This story has been updated to reflect Google's comments.)

data.title: DraftKings and Disney strike a key integration deal ahead of March Madness
data.description:
DraftKings (DKNG) and Disney's (DIS) ESPN announced on Friday that they will enable customers to link their DraftKings Sportsbook and ESPN accounts to deliver a more personalized, streamlined betting and viewing experience. The feature was added just ahead of the men’s and women’s March Madness tournaments, which recently reached their highest viewership in over 30 years. 

Once live, users can link accounts via a new "Bet Your Bracket" tab in the DraftKings (DKNG) app. Fans who create an ESPN Tournament Challenge bracket will then see betting options tailored to their bracket picks, along with customized suggestions and engagement opportunities throughout the tournament, plus one free month of ESPN Unlimited for those who link. 

The move is seen by both companies as a strategic step toward deeper integration, with DraftKings (DKNG) positioning it as part of its upcoming Super App strategy and ESPN highlighting future features such as in-app and web tracking of upcoming, live, and settled bets and personalized promotions based on users’ favorite teams, players, and fantasy rosters. 

Shares of DraftKings (DKNG) fell 1.6% on the day, while ESPN parent Disney (DIS) was down 1.7%.
